items.title: Man remanded over murder of elderly woman
items.summary: IPOH: A man has been remanded for five days to assist investigations into the murder of a 70-year-old woman found dead at her home in Persiaran Pegoh Aman 2, Taman Desa Aman, here last night. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the remand period begins today until Saturday to facilitate investigations. "The 52-year-old man is the deceased's neighbour .

items.title: Heat risks may be underestimated in humid conditions, experts warn
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Experts say Malaysia's reliance on temperature alone may underestimate heat risks, calling for humidity-based heat index measures to better reflect real-world conditions. "Temperature alone does not determine heat stress. He said incorporating heat index-based measurements could improve risk assessments and help authorities better manage exposure, particularly during outdoor activities.

items.title: Two instructors remanded over RM30,000 bribery case
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission has obtained a four-day remand order against two instructors from a state statutory body for allegedly soliciting and accepting RM30,000 in bribes. Magistrate Iffah Nabihah Mohd Ishak issued the remand order until May 8 after the application was made at the Kuala Terengganu magistrate's court today. According to a source, the two male suspects in their 20s and 40s were detained at noon yesterday when they arrived to give their statements at the Terengganu MACC office.

items.title: Drunk driver remanded seven days in murder probe over fatal Jalan Ampang crash that killed married couple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A fatal crash involving a married couple on Jalan Ampang last Saturday is now being investigated as murder. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the 24-year-old male suspect, who was previously receiving treatment at Hospital Kuala Lumpur, has been arrested to assist in the investigation, according to Kosmo! “Today, Kuala Lumpur Senior Assistant Registrar Nur Atikah Zakaria allowed a seven-day remand until May 11 to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Police record over 10 statements in college student murder case
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Police have recorded statements from more than 10 people regarding the death of a college student found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh last Friday. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said those whose statements were recorded include the victim's family and housemates, as well as witnesses. Yusoff said the victim was found without identification documents, but police identified her through a National Registration Department fingerprint check.

items.title: Umno never decided to become opposition in Negri Sembilan, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has never decided to become the opposition in Negri Sembilan and the party will remain in the state unity government, its president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. "Umno at the federal level has already decided that our assemblymen in Negri Sembilan will remain part of the state's unity government," Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said after attending the Kami Gen Mara gathering here today. Also present was Mara chairman and Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: Malaysia's manufacturing outlook holds firm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's continued manufacturing sector expansion in April indicates a relatively firm near-term outlook, according to Kenanga Research. Kenanga Research said this was driven largely by post-festive normalisation and pre-emptive stockbuilding amid supply chain risks linked to ongoing Middle East conflicts. Production, employment and purchasing activity underpinned the rebound, although inflationary pressures climbed to a record high while delivery delays worsened.

items.title: Johari: 20pct helium shortage from Middle East conflict not a serious concern for semiconductor sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A roughly 20 per cent shortage in helium supply stemming from the ongoing Middle East crisis has yet to pose a serious threat to the semiconductor industry, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said. Responding to concerns over potential disruptions to local chip production, Johari said the situation is being closely monitored but has not raised alarm at this stage. Johari said that companies sourcing helium from the Middle East were experiencing a supply shortfall of around 20 per cent as a result of the war.

items.title: Govt declares public holidays for Wesak Day, King's birthday
items.summary: Meanwhile, Wesak Day is a paid public holiday that employers may choose as part of the six paid public holidays that must be granted to employees. "If a public holiday falls on a weekly rest day or any other designated holiday, the next working day shall be observed as a paid replacement public holiday," it said. Accordingly, employers who designate Sunday as the weekly rest day and select Wesak Day as a public holiday must grant June 1 (Monday) as the King's official birthday holiday, which cannot be replaced, and June 2, 2026 (Tuesday), as a replacement holiday for Wesak Day.

items.title: Mysterious world beyond Pluto may have an atmosphere: Astronomers
items.summary: A tiny, little-known world beyond Pluto appears to have an atmosphere, Japanese astronomers said Monday, defying what had been thought possible for icy objects in our cosmic backyard. If confirmed, the roughly 500-kilometre-wide (310-mile) rock would become just the second world past Neptune in our Solar System to host an atmosphere – after only Pluto itself. While NASA under US President Donald Trump has floated the idea of restoring Pluto's planet status, the discovery of another atmosphere nearby could undermine the argument for its reinstatement.

items.title: BN ready if snap polls called in three states, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) is prepared for the possibility of snap elections in Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor, its chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. Zahid, who is also Umno president, said Umno and other BN component parties are ready to face any eventuality in the event the state assemblies in these states are dissolved soon. Asked whether candidates had been identified, Zahid said it was still too early to do so.

items.title: Negri Sembilan Umno hopes for 'fair' resolution to state political crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno hopes that the political bureaus of Umno and Pakatan Harapan will make a fair decision following the recent political crisis in the state. Its chairman, Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, said both party bureaus were fully informed and would consider the best course of action. It was earlier reported that the bureaus would meet at 8pm tonight to discuss and negotiate issues, including the political crisis in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Cameron Highlands floods: TNB ordered to pay RM4.7m over 2013 dam negligence
items.summary: The High Court in Seremban made the award after assessing damages in the case, following earlier findings that TNB had acted negligently in managing the Sultan Abu Bakar Dam, according to Free Malaysia Today. Justice Ahmad Shahrir Salleh granted RM912,459.76 in special damages, alongside RM30,000 in general damages for each plaintiff. In 2018, the Court of Appeal upheld a High Court finding that TNB was negligent in maintaining the dam, and directed that damages be assessed.

items.title: Malaysia allocates three of seven advanced Arm tokens to industry players
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has allocated three of its seven advanced Arm Holdings' integrated circuit (IC) design tokens to industry players on the condition that they collaborate with local companies, International Trade and Investment Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said. An Arm token is essentially a licence that grants a company the right to use the UK tech giant's chip design blueprints. Beyond the seven tokens for the advance Arm Compute Subsystems (CSS), Johari said approximately five companies had received basic Arm Flexible Access (AFA) tokens, with the allocation process still ongoing.

items.title: Man held for beating wife now probed for burying foetus
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the 43-year-old suspect had confessed and pointed out where he buried the foetus on Thursday. He said the foetus belonged to his 39-year-old wife, who had miscarried after he assaulted her at Ambangan Heights, Sungai Petani, on April 2. "Last Thursday, police took the suspect and his wife to the location where the foetus was buried.

items.title: Malaysia sees no major energy, raw material risk to semiconductor industry: Johari Ghani
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia does not see energy supply or raw material shortages as a major risk to its semiconductor industry at this stage, supported by its status as a net gas exporter, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said. Johari said Malaysia currently exports most of its surplus gas, but there is potential to make better domestic use of the resource to support industrial development, including energy-intensive sectors. He said the semiconductor segment remains a key pillar within Malaysia's electrical and electronics (E&E) industry, which recorded exports of about RM711 billion in 2025.

items.title: Ex-model accuses French scout of grooming her for Epstein
items.summary: PARIS: Former French model Juliette G. says it took her years to make sense of how she ended up in a New York bedroom with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. She had no other information, but she felt reassured when her modelling agency said they knew Siad. she said.

items.title: Education Ministry fires back at MCA, says all 10A SPM students got matriculation offers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Education Ministry has refuted claims that several top SPM scorers were denied places in the matriculation programme, saying all eligible 10A students had already been offered admission, according to a Facebook post by Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. Wong said MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong had recently alleged that the Madani government failed to fulfil its promise of offering Matriculation Programme places to 10A SPM high achievers, claiming he had received at least 11 cases of students allegedly left out. On May 4, Wong said Wee later claimed that all 11 students had secured places in the matriculation programme following MCA’s intervention.

items.title: Malaysia's semiconductor sector hits RM400bil in 2025, structural gaps remain, says Johari
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's semiconductor sector posted an estimated revenue of RM400 billion in 2025, a testament to how the industry has grown into a formidable force over a span of five decades, said Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, revealing the figure today. Johari added that Malaysia is pushing to transform its semiconductor industry from a backend-dominated sector into a full-spectrum technology owner, as the global semiconductor market races towards US$2 trillion by 2035. Qualities, he said, Malaysia and the broader Asean region could offer.

items.title: Johor Speaker proposes upgrading UTHM into sports university
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Johor State Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi has proposed that Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia (UTHM) be upgraded to a sports university. Earlier, Datuk Pandak Ahmad (BN-Kota Iskandar) asked Hairi on plans to create a sports university as a development hub in Johor. Hairi said the state government was currently focusing on strengthening the sports development ecosystem through the Johor State Sports Council (MSNJ).

items.title: Teoh Beng Hock murder probe: Family seeks court order for fresh investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The family of the late Teoh Beng Hock has filed a fresh application seeking to compel police to open a murder investigation into his death 17 years ago. He said the family is seeking a court declaration that Teoh’s death should be investigated as murder, as well as an order compelling police to complete the investigation within 120 days of the court order. Ramkarpal said the family had previously written to police asking whether any past investigation papers on Teoh’s death were opened for murder.

items.title: LinkedIn faces complaint over its selling of user data
items.summary: VIENNA: A prominent data protection group on Tuesday filed a complaint in Austria against LinkedIn over the professional networking platform's selling of user data. Vienna-based Noyb – which stands for "None of Your Business" – said it filed a complaint with the Austrian Data Protection Authority on behalf of a LinkedIn user wanting access to his data. "People have the right to receive their own data free of charge," Noyb data protection lawyer Martin Baumann said.

items.title: Man jailed, fined RM5,000 for insulting King on TikTok [WATCH]
items.summary: BANGI: A man was jailed three months and fined RM5,000 by the Bandar Baru Bangi Sessions Court after pleading guilty to posting offensive content insulting His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, on TikTok last year. Judge Firdaus Md Isa ordered Mohd Hafiz Haron, 42, to begin the sentence today and serve an additional four months if he fails to pay the fine. The self-employed father of three was charged with knowingly initiating the transmission of a comment that was grossly offensive in nature, with the intent to hurt others via his TikTok account at 7.30am on Sept 11 last year.

items.title: Tesla faces EU skepticism over automated-driving tech, records show
items.summary: NEW YORK: Tesla chief executive officer Elon Musk has projected confidence that the European Union will soon green-light the carmaker's "Full Self-Driving" (FSD) system, but emails from some European regulators show marked scepticism toward the technology and its stated safety benefits. Tesla's "FSD (Supervised)" technology got the nod from Dutch road regulator RDW in April. RDW is now seeking EU approval for FSD, with a key committee hearing scheduled for Tuesday.

items.title: Man suspected of assaulting wife linked to burial of foetus in Muslim cemetery in Kedah, says police
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said investigations found that the 43-year-old suspect had confessed and led police to the burial site last Thursday, Harian Metro reported today. “Last Thursday, police brought the suspect together with his wife, the 39-year-old victim, to show the location where the foetus was buried. “We also brought a forensic team from the Kedah police headquarters to excavate the site, where we found an undeveloped foetal tissue,” he said at a press conference at the Kedah police headquarters today.

items.title: Suspect in fatal Jalan Ampang crash remanded for seven days
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the suspect was discharged from Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) at 5pm yesterday. "Today at 10.45am, a Kuala Lumpur magistrate's court senior assistant registrar allowed a seven-day remand order until May 11 under Section 302 of the Penal Code," he said in a statement today. Zamzuri said the investigation paper would be referred to the Kuala Lumpur deputy public prosecutor's office soon for further instructions.

items.title: Proposed Mara Act amendments to reach Cabinet earlier than deadline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act 1966 are expected to be tabled to the cabinet within three weeks, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid said this would be earlier than the three-month deadline set by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "Although I was challenged by the prime minister to present it to the cabinet within three months, with everyone's support and preparation from the Mara chairman and Mara director, I will prove that I will present this new Mara Act within three weeks from today.

items.title: Batu Pahat kindergarten teacher fined RM1,200 for pinching four-year-old pupil until bruised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A kindergarten teacher in Batu Pahat has been fined RM1,200 after pleading guilty in the Yong Peng Magistrates’ Court for injuring a four-year-old child under her care by pinching the victim’s arm until it left bruises. Dissatisfied, the mother lodged a police report at Parit Sulong police station. Police later arrested the teacher at the kindergarten to assist investigations.

items.title: Kuala Krai crash: Two killed, two injured in Perodua Kembara and Honda City collision
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Two people were killed and two others injured in a crash involving two vehicles near Simpang 3 Manek Urai Lama earlier this morning. He said the crash involved a Perodua Kembara and a Honda City. “The Honda City driver and a Perodua Kembara passenger were injured,” he said.

items.title: Fake plates used in seizure of 500,000 illicit cigarettes
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Smugglers are using fake vehicle registration numbers to transport illicit cigarettes to evade detection. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said a man was detained in the 9.30pm operation. Ahmad Radzi said checks revealed both vehicles were using fake registration numbers.

items.title: All 14 clinical services at Pasir Gudang Hospital now fully operational
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: All 14 clinical services at the Pasir Gudang Hospital (HPG) are now fully operational, with 157 beds across nine wards ready for use. Johor Health and Environment Committee Chairman Ling Tian Soon said the state government is awaiting the Health Ministry to fill the remaining 59 per cent of vacancies at the new hospital. "HPG is operational.

items.title: Man jailed three months, fined RM5,000 for insulting Yang di-Pertuan Agong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A man has been sentenced to three months’ jail and fined RM5,000 after pleading guilty at the Bandar Baru Bangi Sessions Court for making offensive remarks insulting the Yang di-Pertuan Agong in September last year. The accused, Mohd Hafiz Haron, 42, entered the plea after the charge was read to him before Judge Firdaus Md Isa, Harian Metro reported today. The court sentenced him to three months’ jail starting today and fined RM5,000, in default four months’ jail.

items.title: LA wildfire accused harboured anti-wealth views, supported Mangione
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: The man accused of deliberately setting a deadly fire that torched an upmarket swathe of Los Angeles last year harboured a grudge against the wealthy and admired Luigi Mangione, the man accused of shooting dead an insurance company CEO, federal prosecutors said. In court documents filed last week, prosecutors portray him as an Uber driver outraged by capitalism, who allegedly set fire to the Pacific Palisades neighbourhood – a celebrity enclave where ocean-view villas are worth millions – as an act of revenge. He also searched using the slogan "Free Luigi Mangione" – the man federal prosecutors say gunned down the chief executive of United Healthcare.

items.title: MARA Act amendment ready for Cabinet in three weeks, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A draft amendment to the 1966 Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) Act is set to be tabled before the Cabinet within three weeks, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. With the support and backing of MARA and senior officials, I will present the new MARA Act within three weeks from today. Also present was MARA chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: Two women, including deputy director, remanded over RM15,000 false claims
items.summary: MELAKA: Two women, including a deputy director of a government agency, have been remanded for five days in connection with a false claim for office services and supplies worth RM15,000. Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman allowed the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) application under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code to remand them until May 9 to assist with the investigation. Melaka MACC director Adi Supian Shafie confirmed the arrests when contacted, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: ‘Must look at issue comprehensively’: Perak exco meets Anwar over elephants sent to Japan
items.summary: IPOH, May 5 — Perak Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Sandrea Ng Shy Ching met the Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday to present the latest developments regarding the relocation of three elephants from Zoo Taiping & Night Safari to Tennoji Zoo in Japan. Ng said the meeting, held at the Prime Minister’s Office, discussed several key matters, with particular emphasis on the welfare of the three elephants, namely Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK), as well as the importance of maintaining good diplomatic relations and honouring existing commitments and agreements. The Prime Minister, in a Facebook post yesterday, stressed that the welfare of the three Malaysian elephants must be prioritised by the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES).

items.title: Handal Energy risks trading suspension after missing FY25 Annual Report deadline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Handal Energy Bhd faces a potential trading suspension after it failed to submit its annual report for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25), within the prescribed deadline. In a filing to Bursa Malaysia today, the regulator said the company did not issue its annual report for 2025 by the required deadline of April 30, 2026, under the Main Market listing requirements. Bursa Malaysia said if Handal Energy does not submit the outstanding report by May 8, trading in its securities will be suspended from 9.00am on May 11 until further notice.

items.title: Taiwan leader Lai says island has 'right to engage with the world'
items.summary: TAOYUAN: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te said on Tuesday the democratic island has the "right to engage with the world", after he returned from a trip to Eswatini, Taipei's only African ally. "Taiwanese people are people of the world; Taiwanese people have the right to engage with the world," Lai told reporters at Taipei's international airport after his flight on the Eswatini king's plane. Lai flew on the king's plane to Eswatini on Saturday.

items.title: Kindergarten teacher fined RM1,200 for causing hurt to 4-year-old girl [WATCH]
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A kindergarten teacher was fined RM1,200 by the magistrate's court in Yong Peng today after pleading guilty to injuring a 4-year-old girl under her care last month. Nurul Amira Natasya Mohd Nazri, 25, entered the plea before magistrate Arun Noval Dass. Following the report, police arrested Nurul Amira at the kindergarten.

items.title: Suspects in fatal Jalan Ampang accident discharged from hospital
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said police have since recorded the statement of one of them to assist in investigations. "However, we have yet to record the driver's statement," he told the New Straits Times. Zamzuri said this was because the driver is still unwell following the crash.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng slams BN, Umno, PN, PAS over Rain Rave criticism, cites RM200m tourism boost
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — DAP Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng has criticised BN, Umno, Perikatan Nasional (PN) and PAS for opposing the Rain Rave Water Festival 2026, saying they should be “ashamed” after the event reportedly drew 180,000 visitors and generated up to RM200 million in tourism revenue. Lim said the earnings would benefit small traders while also strengthening Malaysia’s image as a safe destination for international and domestic tourism. Lim also cited broader sector performance, saying Malaysia recorded RM332 billion in tourism revenue and 42 million tourist arrivals in 2025.

items.title: Rafizi says has ‘nothing to hide’ in RM1.1b probe, declines further comment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said he has “nothing to hide” over investigations linked to a RM1.1 billion investment project, but declined to comment further as the matter remains under probe. The Pandan MP made the remarks when met at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here today, where he was present to continue giving his statement, according to Harian Metro . “Nothing to hide,” he said.

items.title: Woman, 70, found dead in suspected hacking incident in Ipoh, machete found at scene
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A 70-year-old woman was found dead in what police believe to be a murder case at a house in Taman Desa Aman in Ipoh last night. According to BuletinTV3, Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the victim was found lying motionless and was confirmed dead by medical officers. Police have advised the public against speculating on the case to avoid disrupting investigations and urged those with information to contact the Ipoh district police headquarters or the nearest police station.

items.title: Muhammad Faris Johari installed as 11th Undang Luak Sungai Ujong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Muhammad Faris Johari has been installed as the Yamtuan Muda (YTM) Dato’ Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungai Ujong, becoming the 11th holder of the traditional office in Negeri Sembilan. According to a statement posted by Majlis Dato Lembaga Adat Sungai Ujong on Facebook today, the appointment was formalised following a customary selection process involving royal and adat stakeholders of the Luak Sungai Ujong community. The installation ceremony took place at Telaga Undang, Luak Sungai Ujong, Batu 9, Kampung Telaga Undang in Pantai, Negeri Sembilan, on April 22, 2026, in accordance with longstanding Adat Perpatih traditions.

items.title: KL woman slips into river during hike, found dead in Gua Musang
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 5 — A single mother in her 50s drowned after she is believed to have slipped into Sungai Betis while hiking in Pos Balar, Gua Musang. Gua Musang Police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said the woman from Kuala Lumpur was part of a group of 11 hikers who began their expedition on May 1. He said the expedition covered the areas of Gunung Gerah, Gunung Bilah and Gunung Biyed.

items.title: DAP backs Aminuddin to stay MB but still prepares for snap polls in Negeri, Melaka, Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — DAP is putting its entire party machinery on standby to face the possibility of snap elections in Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, and Johor, the party announced today following a high-level meeting. In a statement following its monthly Central Executive Committee (CEC) meeting, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke also reaffirmed the party’s complete support for incumbent Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. “DAP will continue to support and give its full backing to Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as menteri besar of Negeri Sembilan,” Loke said.

items.title: Tiger stop diplomacy: Singapore senior minister Lee Hsien Loong kicks off Pahang visit at conservation centre
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 5 — Singapore Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong began his official visit to Pahang yesterday by visiting the Malayan Tiger Conservation Centre (MTCC) in Lanchang, Temerloh. Also present were Temerloh District Officer Datuk Khairur Rizal Ramli and Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) Peninsular Malaysia deputy director-general (conservation) Datuk Fakhrul Hatta Musa. Lee is on a five-day visit to Pahang and Terengganu from today until May 8.

items.title: ‘True heroes’ in action: Anwar salutes firefighters’ life-saving role on global day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday expressed his highest appreciation to all firefighters for their significant role in minimising national losses through their speed and efficiency in handling emergencies. “The speed and efficiency of firefighters in executing their mandate and responsibilities have successfully minimised damage, thereby supporting the nation’s continued development and well-being,” he said in a Facebook post yesterday in conjunction with International Firefighters’ Day. “When a fire occurs, we call the fire department.

items.title: Umno at 80: Party veep says party must evolve or risk fading relevance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — As Umno marks its 80th anniversary, the party remains relevant in Malaysia’s political landscape but must be more responsive and agile in addressing public views and criticism, said its Vice-President Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. He said the party is no longer as dominant as before, requiring a more mature and renewed approach in line with shifting political dynamics and a new generation of voters with different expectations. Wan Rosdy said Umno must not take criticism lightly, but instead carefully assess public feedback and translate it into improvements.

items.title: Kedah rice delays: Mada blames water shortage, erratic weather
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 5 — The Muda Agricultural Development Authority (MADA) appeared to explain the real situation regarding the delay in rice planting in Kedah, while emphasising that the institution is always closely monitoring the situation of padi fields, which are affected by the lack of water supply and increasing operating costs. According to him, the water supply for irrigating padi fields depends on four main sources, namely dams, rivers, rain and water recycling, with approximately 50 per cent depending on dam storage. “The three main dams, namely the Muda Dam, the Pedu Dam and the Ahning Dam, are important sources, but the current level is insufficient to release water for irrigation purposes.

items.title: Technical issue grounds MH8110: 278 pilgrims from KT051 to resume Haj journey on replacement flight via Muscat
items.summary: MECCA, May 5 — A total of 278 Malaysian Haj pilgrims on flight KT051, which landed in Muscat, Oman following a technical issue, are scheduled to continue their journey to Jeddah early Wednesday on an alternative aircraft. Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said in a statement today that the flight operated by AMAL by Malaysia Airlines is scheduled to depart Muscat International Airport on May 6 at 3am (Oman time). The aircraft, MH8110, which departed Kuala Lumpur International Airport for Jeddah, landed in Muscat at 4.11pm local time yesterday following the technical issue.

items.title: Medicine supply jitters: Health Ministry forms special task force to secure pipeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is strengthening the country’s preparedness in the face of global supply chain uncertainties by establishing a Special Task Force on Drug and Medical Device Safety as the main control centre. Its minister, Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the move was taken in response to the global dynamic challenges involving the raw material supply crisis, energy crisis, and the increasing surge in logistics costs worldwide following the tensions in West Asia. He said the team was currently implementing real-time data monitoring in addition to diversifying sources of drug procurement from the international market to ensure the country’s supply remained stable.

items.title: Sabah’s 40pc revenue claim gains traction with structured federal-state talks, says minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 5 — Negotiations between the federal government and the Sabah state government on the state’s 40 per cent revenue entitlement claim are actively progressing, focusing on four key aspects, said Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. The Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) said the first focus is to finalise the calculation formula based on the federal government’s net revenue derived from Sabah, in line with Articles 112C and 112D of the Federal Constitution. He said the second focus involves the alignment and verification of revenue data involving key agencies such as the Inland Revenue Board and the Royal Malaysian Customs Department, while the third aspect is to fairly address long-standing unresolved past payments, or “lost years”.

items.title: RM1.1b probe: Rafizi scheduled back at MACC at 10am today as questioning rolls into second day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 5 — Former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli is scheduled to return to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here today to continue providing his statement in connection with an investigation into a RM1.1 billion investment case. “It is a normal investigation process, they ask questions, and I answer. Meanwhile, Mohd Hafaz, in a separate statement, said Rafizi’s presence was to provide the commission with cooperation in completing its ongoing investigation.

items.title: ‘Allah’ inscription at Perlis shrine: Shop owner freed on bail as probe continues
items.summary: Kangar district police chief Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said the man was released earlier yesterday. “Police take a serious view of any act that could threaten public order and will take firm action against any individual involved,” he said in a statement yesterday. Perlis police chief Datuk Muhammad Abdul Halim had earlier told Bernama that police received a report at 2.30 pm yesterday after a man watched a video of the incident on the Facebook page “SAYA ANAK PERLIS”.

items.title: Mid-air scare averted: MH8110 diverts to Muscat, all 278 Haj pilgrims safe
items.summary: MECCA, May 5 — Flight MH8110, carrying 278 KT051 Haj pilgrims, made a precautionary landing at Muscat International Airport in Oman yesterday following a technical issue. Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said the aircraft landed at 4.11pm local time, and all pilgrims are safe and unaffected. “The landing was a standard safety measure to ensure the pilgrims’ journey proceeded smoothly and safely.

items.title: Simplified: Why Tuanku Muhriz is legally still the Negeri Sembilan ruler, and why Undangs’ attempt to ‘sack’ him is invalid, experts say
items.summary: The four “Undangs” or ruling chiefs of Negeri Sembilan have to follow the procedures in the state’s Constitution, legal experts said. Based on the Negeri Sembilan State Constitution, the Undangs’ recent attempt to “sack” Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir as Negeri Sembilan’s Yang di-Pertuan Besar is invalid. KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Over the past few weeks, Negeri Sembilan was rocked by an attempt by four traditional ruling chiefs (“Undangs”) to remove the state ruler Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.

items.title: Semporna waters drama: Suspect dives into sea as MMEA seizes 575kg fertiliser for fish bombs
items.summary: SEMPORNA, May 4 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) seized 575 kilogrammes (kg) of ammonia fertiliser believed to be used to produce fish bombs in a boat abandoned in the waters of Pulau Bum Bum here yesterday. MMEA Semporna maritime zone director Cmdr (M) Amir Shubli said the seizure at 4 m occurred after a patrol detected a suspcious pump boat at 0.2 nautical miles north of the island and saw a suspect jump into the sea. “A suspect escaped by swimming to the shore nearby, and inspections of the boat revealed 22 sacks of ammonia fertiliser with an estimated weight of about 575kg.

items.title: Penang confirms SOP compliance after runner dies of heatstroke in 30km hill run
items.summary: Penang Youth, Sports, and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the organisers had provided all essential requirements, including sufficient water stations and medical teams along the route, and had acted promptly when the victim was found to be unwell. The Penang State Health Department (JKNPP) reported yesterday that the victim died on April 26 following his participation in the run. In another development, Gooi said Penang remains free from monkey malaria (Plasmodium knowlesi) infections despite a spike in cases in Sabah.

items.title: PM Anwar orders urgent cloud seeding in Kedah, Perlis, calls for drones to overcome aircraft shortage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) to expedite cloud seeding operations in states currently at critical levels, particularly Kedah and Perlis. Apart from weather issues, the meeting also discussed the welfare of three national elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat – currently in Japan. Anwar also reminded the ministry on matters of management and governance.
